mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, with a cumulative abnormal return indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return over 60 days in backtested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings revisions, creating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
